title: Family Vocabulary & Adjective Agreement
description: Learn family member nouns and how adjectives agree with noun gender

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct Czech word for 'family'

data.question_en: What is the Czech word for 'family'?
data.explanation: 'Rodina' is a feminine noun meaning 'family'. It is the nominative singular form.

exercises.prompt: Match the family members to their English meanings

data.explanation: 'Dědeček' means 'grandfather'. It is a masculine animate noun. 'Babička' is 'grandmother'.

exercises.prompt: Identify the correct adjective form for a masculine noun

data.explanation: 'Otec' is masculine animate, so the adjective takes the masculine ending '-ý': dobrý otec (good father).

exercises.prompt: Identify the correct adjective form for a feminine noun

data.explanation: 'Matka' is feminine, so the adjective takes the feminine ending '-á': dobrá matka (good mother).

data.grammar_note: 'Rodina' is feminine. In the accusative case (object of 'mít'), the adjective ending becomes '-ou': velkou rodinu. 'Velký' is the masculine form, 'velká' is feminine nominative, but here we need accusative.
'Sestra' is feminine, so 'malý' (masc) becomes 'malá' (fem). 'Moje' is the possessive 'my/mine' used for feminine and neuter nouns.
'Můj' is the masculine possessive 'my'. 'Bratr' is masculine animate. Czech does not use an indefinite article, so 'student' translates as 'a student'.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct adjective form

data.explanation: 'Dům' (house) is masculine inanimate. The adjective 'starý' (old) takes the masculine ending '-ý': starý dům.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct adjective form

data.explanation: 'Kniha' (book) is feminine. The adjective 'nový' (new) takes the feminine ending '-á': nová kniha. 'Moje' is used with feminine and neuter nouns.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence

data.explanation: Word order: Subject (Moje sestra) + verb (je) + adjective predicate (hezká). 'Sestra' is feminine, so 'hezký' becomes 'hezká'.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear

data.expected_text: Babička je stará a dědeček je také starý.
data.note: Listen for the feminine adjective ending '-á' on 'stará' (referring to babička) and the masculine ending '-ý' on 'starý' (referring to dědeček).

exercises.answer_key: Babička je stará a dědeček je také starý.
